expectations.text: Guide follows four-segment structure (Opening, Warm-up, Core Discovery, Wrap-up)
expectations.evidence: Four segments present: SEGMENT 1: OPENING (5 minutes), SEGMENT 2: WARM-UP (5-10 minutes), SEGMENT 3: CORE DISCOVERY (20-25 minutes), SEGMENT 4: WRAP-UP (5 minutes)

expectations.text: Questions ask about past behaviour, not hypothetical futures
expectations.evidence: Core discovery opens with 'Can you walk me through the last time you brought someone new onto your team'. Depth questions follow: 'What happened on their first day?', 'What was the hardest part?'. No hypothetical questions like 'Would you use...' appear in the discovery section.

expectations.text: No mention of the proposed onboarding feature in the first half of the guide
expectations.evidence: The opening script says 'I'm interested in how you bring new people onto your team, not in testing you on anything.' No mention of a product feature in Segments 1-3. The Interviewer Reminders explicitly state: 'Do NOT mention the onboarding feature idea until after Core Discovery is complete.'

Explicit workaround probe in Segment 3: 'Before you had your current process — or if you think back to the very first time you onboarded someone here — how did you handle it? [Wait] And do you still use any part of that earlier approach today?'

expectations.text: Note-taking template includes Key observations, Notable quotes, Surprises, Follow-up questions
expectations.evidence: Note-taking template includes all four: 'Key observations:', 'Notable quotes:', 'Surprises / unexpected:', 'Follow-up questions:'. Also adds 'Workarounds in use:' as a bonus field.

expectations.text: Guide specifies participant criteria and target sample size
expectations.evidence: Header specifies: 'Participants: People managers and HR/People Ops leads at companies with 50-500 employees who have onboarded at least 2 new hires in the past 6 months; n=8-10 target'
